title: Mammillaria rekoiana
taxonomy: Mammillaria rekoiana belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a species of flowering cactus. Mammillaria rekoiana is classified under the genus Mammillaria, which includes around 200 species of cacti. The species is native to Mexico and is characterized by its white or pink flowers and globular shape. Mammillaria rekoiana is a popular ornamental plant among cactus enthusiasts due to its compact size and unique appearance.
uses: Mammillaria rekoiana has been researched for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in the treatment of various health issues. The plant's extracts have shown promise in exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These properties may contribute to its potential in combating infections, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Additionally, Mammillaria rekoiana has been studied for its possible antidiabetic and anticancer properties. However, further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal applications and potential benefits.

side_effects: Mammillaria rekoiana is a non-toxic and relatively safe cactus species for humans and pets. However, like all cacti, its spines can cause irritation and minor injuries if handled improperly. The plant's sap may cause skin irritation in some individuals, although this is rare. When ingested, Mammillaria rekoiana is not toxic but can cause gastrointestinal upset in humans and pets. If ingested, it's essential to seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
